diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with a clinical presentation typical for ataxia with oculomotor apraxia type 1, including childhood onset of slowly progressive cerebellar ataxia, oculomotor apraxia, dysarthria, limb dysmetria, and motor and sensory axonal neuropathy. Molecular genetic testing identified a homozygous deletion of all coding APTX exons (3 to 9), confirming the diagnosis of AOA1. The reasoning included clinical symptoms, imaging showing cerebellar atrophy, and genetic testing results.
